title: Hepetis tuerckheimii
taxonomy: Hepetis tuerckheimii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is a member of the genus Heptis, which is a small group of plants. The plant is native to Central America, specifically in the countries of Guatemala and Mexico. Within the family Lamiaceae, Hepetis tuerckheimii is further classified as a type of mint or mint-like plant. Its classification is based on its physical characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants in the Lamiaceae family.

intro: Hepetis tuerckheimii, also known as Wild Dagga, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, belonging to the Lamiaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues, due to its sedative and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Wild Dagga include cannabinoids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into smoking blends, although its use as a psychoactive substance is largely limited to its historical context.
